text.title: outcome-based-roadmaps Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for outcome-based-roadmaps as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Outcome rewording: translating "Build chat" to "Improve communication outcomes." The words change but no metric, baseline, or target is added. The row is still a feature in disguise.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Outcome framing requires a measurable metric movement, not a rephrasing. "Improve communication outcomes" has no baseline and no target, making it untraceable and un-kill-criterion-able.

failure-mode.description: Single-solution rows: "Reduce churn from 8% to 5% by building a re-engagement email sequence." One solution means the team has already decided the implementation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The value of outcome framing is preserving solution flexibility. A single committed solution collapses outcome framing back into feature roadmap behavior. Require at least 2 candidate solutions to maintain that flexibility.

failure-mode.description: Unbounded horizons: "Improve retention" with no time target. Teams can claim progress at any point without accountability.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An outcome without a horizon is not a commitment. "Improve retention to 40% DAU/MAU" is a vibe until "by end of Q2" is added. All outcome rows require explicit horizon specification.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated baselines: outcome rows that list a baseline number that was never measured, because the metric is not yet instrumented.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Invented baselines destroy tracking credibility. If the metric is not instrumented, the row must say "TO BE INSTRUMENTED, owner=X" — never an invented number. Teams cannot improve a metric they cannot measure.

failure-mode.description: Weekly roadmap rewrites. The agent re-translates the entire roadmap every week, losing the commitment history and making it impossible to track whether outcomes are improving.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Outcome roadmaps should be updated at two cadences: (1) weekly metric monitor for progress tracking; (2) quarterly re-decomposition for strategic revision. Rewriting weekly destroys the accountability record.

failure-mode.description: Stakeholders silently translating outcome rows back to feature commitments. Leadership hears "reduce support tickets by 30%" and immediately asks "which features will you ship to do that?" and anchors on the answer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This is an ongoing communication problem, not a one-time fix. PMs must actively police the framing in every stakeholder interaction, maintaining a "confirmed solutions" list as a strict subset of the roadmap for external timing commitments.

failure-mode.description: LLMs translate features to outcomes by simply rewording ("Build chat" → "Improve communication outcomes"). That is not an outcome. Force a measurable metric movement per row.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rephrasing is easier than finding metrics. Models lack the domain knowledge to identify outcome-relevant KPIs.

failure-mode.description: Agents prefer 1 confident solution per outcome ("the obvious answer"). Force a minimum of 2 candidate solutions to preserve solution discovery.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Models optimize for coherent single narratives, not exploring solution space.

failure-mode.description: Vague outcome verbs (improve, enhance, optimize) creep in. Add a banned-verb instruction or require numeric movement per outcome.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vague language is natural to language models; specificity requires explicit constraint.

failure-mode.description: View-generator agents drift between leadership and engineering views — same row says different things. Require traceability ID per row across views.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Models optimize for each-view coherence independently, not cross-view consistency.

failure-mode.description: Agents hallucinate baselines. If the metric is not instrumented, the row must say "TO BE INSTRUMENTED, owner=X" — never invented numbers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Plausible numbers are easier to generate than admitting measurement gaps.

failure-mode.description: Human-in-loop checkpoint: outcome decomposition (goal → outcomes) is strategic; humans must own the final tree before any view is generated.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Strategic decomposition requires human judgment about impact and importance.

failure-mode.description: Human-in-loop checkpoint: kill criteria require human sign-off — agents are too quick to delete outcomes the moment metrics wobble.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Models over-optimize for "success" signals; kill criteria require human judgment about acceptable risk.

failure-mode.description: Do not let the agent re-rewrite the roadmap weekly. Cadence: weekly metric monitor, quarterly re-decomposition.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Frequent rewrites destroy accountability and commitment history.
